verbatim_text: And finally, I'm going to talk about benchmarking research agents. And that will be the final part of the talk today. Okay. So let's jump into the first one. In this first one, where we talk about training recipes for reasoning, I will tell you about a recent work of mine called T-squared scaling, where we created a scaling law that took into account deployment and pre-training considerations. So how do we get training recipes at all? We get them from scaling laws. And I want to walk us through an analogy for scaling laws so that we can understand what we're talking about here. Suppose you are a baker, but you're very bad at your job and you don't know how to bake a cake. And you're tasked with baking a ginormous wedding cake that's going to take a lot of your resources to create. So scaling laws are much like baking a wedding cake. You have perhaps different amounts of flour and eggs and butter and milk and so forth. I don't know how to bake a cake. You have some ingredients and you need some ratio of these ingredients in order to bake it.
